summary: DHH announces that Dave Thomas, author of the Pragmatic Programmer, has committed to writing an introduction to Rails as part of a new 'Facets of Ruby' book series from the Pragmatic Bookshelf. The series will include multiple focused technical books about different aspects of Ruby. DHH expresses excitement about the validation this brings to Rails and the broader Ruby ecosystem. He forecasts 2005 as a breakout year for Ruby, with Rails 1.0, RubyGems in the standard library, and multiple books on the horizon.
key_points: Dave Thomas announced he is writing an introductory Rails book for the Pragmatic Bookshelf
The Pragmatic Bookshelf is launching a 'Facets of Ruby' series of focused technical books
Rails 1.0, RubyGems inclusion in the standard library, and new books are all converging in 2005
DHH frames 2005 as a pivotal moment and invites developers to join the Ruby community
money_quotes: I can't say how happy I am to be involved with a project that contains the words 'book', 'Dave Thomas', and 'Ruby on Rails'.
Ruby is headed for intensely interesting times in 2005.
I couldn't imagine any development ecosystem I'd rather be a part of.
The Ruby community is a delightfully friendly bunch that loves to share their enthusiasm for this beautiful and seductive language.
And it's just the right time for you to get on board as well.
key_insight: The convergence of major book deals, Rails 1.0, and growing ecosystem tooling signaled that Ruby was about to break into the mainstream developer consciousness.
